records.ety: [OE. bargayn, bargany, OF. bargaigne, bargagne, prob. from a supposed LL. barcaneum, fr. barca a boat which carries merchandise to the shore; hence, to traffic to and fro, to carry on commerce in general. See Bark a vessel. ]

defs: An agreement between parties concerning the sale of property; or a contract by which one party binds himself to transfer the right to some property for a consideration, and the other party binds himself to receive the property and pay the consideration.
quotes: A contract is a bargain that is legally binding.

defs: An agreement or stipulation; mutual pledge.
quotes: And whon your honors mean to solemnize The bargain of your faith.

defs: A purchase; also ( when not qualified), a gainful transaction; an advantageous purchase; as, to buy a thing at a bargain.
The thing stipulated or purchased; also, anything bought cheap.
quotes: She was too fond of her most filthy bargain.

records.ety: [OE. barganien, OF. bargaigner, F. barguigner, to hesitate, fr. LL. barcaniare. See Bargain, n.]

defs: To make a bargain; to make a contract for the exchange of property or services; -- followed by with and for; as, to bargain with a farmer for a cow.
quotes: So worthless peasants bargain for their wives.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bargained (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Bargaining.]

defs: To transfer for a consideration; to barter; to trade; as, to bargain one horse for another.
